questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor (W-KNN) algorithm, and how does it differ from the standard K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
questions.hint: Describe the differences in the decision-making process of W-KNN and KNN.

questions.question: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of using the distance metric (e.g., Euclidean, Manhattan, Minkowski) in the Nearest-Neighbor algorithm. Provide examples of when each distance metric might be suitable.
questions.hint: Consider the characteristics of different datasets and the types of problems being solved.

questions.question: Suppose we are using the W-KNN algorithm to classify images into two classes. If we want to give more weight to the nearest neighbors that are closer to the test instance, what value of k should we choose for the weighted average of the class labels?
questions.hint: Think about how the weights are assigned and how they affect the final classification.
